solutions.title: Solutions
solutions.subtitle: Professional smart energy management solutions for various industries

hems.title: Residential Energy Storage
hems.description: Integrated energy management for residential PV, battery storage, and EV charging — maximizing green energy self-consumption and reducing electricity costs.
hems.pain1: High bills: Peak-hour electricity costs are significant
hems.pain2: Instability: Power outages affect quality of life
hems.pain3: No coordination: Devices cannot work together intelligently
hems.feature1: Peak-valley arbitrage: Auto charge at low rates, discharge at peak
hems.feature2: Smart scheduling: Optimize based on weather and usage patterns
hems.feature3: Backup power: Seamless off-grid switching, zero interruption
hems.feature4: Cloud Direct Access: No additional gateway hardware required; devices connect directly via cloud APIs for rapid, low-cost deployment.
hems.background: Households have installed photovoltaic + energy storage systems, but the devices come from different brands with varied protocols, lacking a unified monitoring and dispatch interface. Complex peak/off-peak electricity pricing policies make manual management unable to maximize energy-saving benefits.
hems.case0Scale: Multi-household PV + ESS systems with various brands
hems.case0Problem: 35% electricity cost reduction after unified integration, self-consumption rate increased to 72%
hems.case1Scale: 3.5kW PV + 5kWh ESS integrated with local peak/off-peak tariff structure
hems.case1Problem: Automatic electricity usage strategy switching, saving approximately 180 AUD per month

ess.title: Commercial & Industrial Storage
ess.description: Help factories, malls and offices reduce total electricity costs by 20–40% through demand management and peak-valley arbitrage.
ess.pain1: High demand charges: Peak usage spikes demand fees
ess.pain2: Missed arbitrage: Unable to exploit peak-valley price spreads
ess.pain3: Mixed fleet: Multiple storage brands with no unified management
ess.feature1: Demand control: Real-time power monitoring to prevent overages
ess.feature2: Arbitrage engine: Identify optimal charge/discharge timing automatically
ess.feature3: Multi-brand: Manage all storage brands from one platform
ess.feature4: Multi-site Unified Management: Centralized monitoring of multiple energy storage sites, improving operational efficiency by 80% with tiered alarm notifications.
ess.background: Factories, commercial buildings, and campuses face high pressure from large peak/off-peak electricity price differences and high electricity costs. While energy storage systems are a core cost-saving solution, managing distributed multi-site setups is complex, and integrating cross-brand equipment is challenging.
ess.case0Problem: Cloud platform deployment completed in 5 days, cross-brand device integration achieved in 10 days
ess.case1Problem: Annual peak-valley arbitrage revenue of 800,000 RMB, O&M efficiency improved by 80%

evcms.title: EV Charging Station
evcms.description: Complete solution for charging station operators: multi-charger coordination, dynamic load balancing (DLB), billing management — maximize throughput while protecting grid safety.
evcms.pain1: Trip risk: Multiple simultaneous charges overload transformers
evcms.pain2: Complex ops: Multiple charger brands hard to manage
evcms.pain3: Low revenue: Inefficient power allocation hurts operations
evcms.feature2: OCPP protocol: Compatible with major charger brands
evcms.feature3: Smart billing: Flexible pricing strategies and membership management
evcms.feature4: Real-time Monitoring & Operational Data: One-stop visual management of charging records, fault alarms, and power curves.
evcms.background: Parking lots, office buildings, and corporate campuses need to deploy multiple charging piles, but main line capacity is limited. Simultaneous fast charging by multiple piles can easily trip the main circuit breaker. Mixed charging pile brands and varying protocols make unified management difficult.
evcms.case0Area: 🇨🇳 Office building parking lot in Luohu, Shenzhen
evcms.case0Scale: 40 charging piles, original main line 150kW, no capacity expansion
evcms.case0Problem: Trip rate reduced to 0 after DLB deployment, charging throughput increased by 60%
evcms.case1Scale: Mixed deployment of multi-brand OCPP charging piles
evcms.case1Problem: Unified management platform launched, O&M labor costs reduced by 55%

vpp.title: Virtual Power Plant
vpp.description: Aggregate distributed storage, PV and EV chargers into a virtual power plant to participate in grid ancillary services and generate additional revenue.
vpp.pain1: Idle assets: Storage assets are underutilized
vpp.pain2: Policy complexity: How to enter electricity markets is unclear
vpp.pain3: High barrier: VPP technical integration is complex
vpp.feature1: Resource aggregation: Unified dispatch of distributed energy
vpp.feature2: Market access: Connect to major power spot markets and ancillary services
vpp.feature3: Revenue analytics: Real-time arbitrage calculation with transparent settlement
vpp.feature4: Multiple Electricity Market Access: Supports various trading models such as spot markets and peak-shaving/frequency regulation ancillary services.
vpp.background: Dispersed energy assets cannot individually participate in grid dispatch. VPP aggregates a large number of distributed batteries, coordinating them into a unified 'virtual power plant' that collectively discharges during peak hours and stores energy during off-peak hours, participating in electricity spot markets and demand response mechanisms to generate revenue.
vpp.case0Scale: Aggregating multi-brand residential energy storage devices to participate in FCAS demand response
vpp.case0Problem: Dispatch commands issued within 500ms, asset utilization significantly improved
vpp.case1Problem: First-year revenue of 2 million RMB, response time < 2s

pqms.title: Power Quality Management
pqms.description: Comprehensive power quality monitoring and mitigation — detect harmonics, voltage imbalance, and power factor issues in real time to protect sensitive equipment.
pqms.pain1: Equipment damage: Harmonic pollution causes frequent failures
pqms.pain2: High consumption: Low power factor increases reactive losses
pqms.pain3: Hard to trace: Power quality issues are difficult to diagnose
pqms.feature1: 24/7 monitoring: Continuous power quality data collection
pqms.feature2: Harmonic analysis: Identify harmonic sources with remediation guidance
pqms.feature3: Alerting: Real-time anomaly push notifications, proactive prevention
pqms.feature4: Multi-level Alarm Notifications: Real-time alerts via Enterprise WeChat, email, and SMS for early warning of abnormalities to prevent issues.
pqms.background: Industrial parks, data centers, hospitals, and commercial complexes have stringent requirements for power quality. Issues like harmonic pollution, voltage sags, and three-phase imbalance directly affect equipment lifespan and production safety, but traditional manual inspection methods are slow and lack comprehensive coverage.
pqms.case0Scale: THD > 15%, frequent failures of precision equipment
pqms.case0Problem: Accurate harmonic source localization, equipment failure rate reduced by 78%
pqms.case1Scale: Voltage fluctuations in critical loads, manual inspections lagging
pqms.case1Problem: 7×24 real-time monitoring, early warning of 3 potential faults, reducing on-site inspections by 90%

products.title: Products
products.subtitle: High-performance new energy management hardware and software

neuron-ii.title: Neuron II
neuron-ii.description: Neuron II is a universal multi-protocol smart gateway supporting 4G, WiFi, RS485, CAN and other interfaces, widely applicable to energy storage, PV, power quality and more. Works offline without cloud dependency.
neuron-ii.spec_protocols: Modbus RTU/TCP, OCPP 1.6J, MQTT, HTTP REST

neuron-iii.title: Neuron III
neuron-iii.tagline: EV Charging Controller with Built-in Meter + DLB
neuron-iii.description: Neuron III is a smart controller designed for EV charging stations, featuring built-in high-accuracy meter and millisecond DLB (Dynamic Load Balancing) for multi-charger coordination and anti-trip protection.

neuron-iii-lite.title: Neuron III Lite
neuron-iii-lite.description: Neuron III Lite is a lightweight anti-trip controller for small charging scenarios, paired with ATP III to provide basic DLB preventing circuit breaker trips.

hems.title: HEMS
hems.description: HEMS provides intelligent energy scheduling for residential energy storage, optimizing PV-storage-charging integration to maximize green energy self-consumption and reduce electricity bills.
hems.positioning: Unified Management for Home PV, Storage, and Charging
hems.swScenarioText: Supports comprehensive management services for new energy power stations, including data acquisition, station monitoring, operation, and maintenance for photovoltaic, energy storage, and other new energy sources. Through a cloud-based big data analytics platform, it helps users achieve transparent management, automated operation and maintenance, intelligent diagnostics, and decision support for all their new energy power stations. It fully meets users' needs at various levels throughout the power station lifecycle, maximizing station value and protecting users' core assets.
hems.swFeature0Desc: Based on a dual architecture for network attack defense and data privacy protection, it creates a trusted operating environment and a closed-loop for asset security in the energy management system.
hems.swFeature0Point0: Financial-grade security protection and access control
hems.swFeature0Point2: Compliance certifications for domestic and international standards
hems.swFeature1Desc: Comprehensive coverage from component-level to station-level to batch diagnostics, with precise guidance for maintenance, simplifying O&M tasks and improving efficiency.
hems.swFeature2Desc: Advanced algorithms for intelligent scheduling, multiple energy modes to choose from, and easy green power management at your fingertips.
hems.swFeature2Point1: AI energy management, intelligent and efficient
hems.swFeature2Point2: Customizable dashboard, full control at your fingertips

ess.title: ESS
ess.description: ESS targets commercial and industrial energy storage, supporting peak-valley arbitrage, demand management, and backup power applications.
ess.positioning: Multi-site Monitoring for Commercial & Industrial Energy Storage
ess.swScenarioText: Can be used for unified monitoring and dispatch of energy storage assets across multiple sites such as factories, commercial buildings, and parks. Enables cross-brand device integration, automatic peak-valley arbitrage, and demand management, reducing overall electricity costs by 20~40%.
ess.swFeature0Desc: Manage all energy storage sites from a single platform, view real-time SOC, power, and health status of each site. Increases operational efficiency by 80% with hierarchical alarm notifications.
ess.swFeature0Point0: Multi-site overview on a single screen
ess.swFeature1Desc: Automatically identifies optimal charging and discharging times, charging during low-price periods and discharging during high-price periods to maximize revenue from electricity price differentials. The strategy engine dynamically responds to real-time price fluctuations.
ess.swFeature1Point0: Dynamic response to real-time electricity prices
ess.swFeature1Point1: Automatic execution of optimal charge/discharge strategies
ess.swFeature2Desc: Real-time monitoring of power demand, intelligent peak shaving and valley filling to avoid exceeding high demand charges, eliminating the need for costly transformer capacity expansion.
ess.swFeature2Point1: Automatic peak shaving and valley filling scheduling
ess.swFeature2Point2: Significant reduction in capacity expansion costs
ess.swFeature3Desc: Daily/Weekly/Monthly reports are automatically generated, supporting export to Excel/PDF. Complete energy consumption and revenue analysis to assist operational decision-making.

evcms.title: EVCMS
evcms.description: EVCMS provides full lifecycle EV charging station management, supporting multi-charger coordination, dynamic load balancing, and OCPP protocol integration.
evcms.positioning: Multi-pile Load Balancing for Charging Stations
evcms.swScenarioText: Can be used for dynamic load balancing and operational management of multi-pile charging stations in parking lots, office buildings, campuses, etc., enabling coordinated multi-pile charging and smart billing without expanding the main power line capacity.
evcms.swFeature0Desc: Millisecond-level real-time detection of the total main line load, dynamically allocating available power to each pile. Supports more charging piles operating simultaneously without expanding the main line, completely preventing tripping.
evcms.swFeature0Point1: Zero expansion supports multi-pile simultaneous charging
evcms.swFeature1Desc: Supports OCPP 1.6J / 2.0.1, compatible with mainstream charging pile brands on the market. No need to replace existing hardware, enabling quick integration for unified management.
evcms.swFeature1Point2: No need to replace existing equipment
evcms.swFeature2Desc: Supports various billing strategies such as time-based billing, energy-based billing, and member discounts. Features one-click settlement and supports VIP priority mode and queue mode.
evcms.swFeature2Point0: Flexible configuration of multi-dimensional billing strategies
evcms.swFeature3Desc: Real-time visualization of charging records, fault alarms, and power curves. One-stop access to full-site operational data, helping maximize site efficiency.

pqms.title: PQMS
pqms.description: PQMS monitors power grid quality in real time, providing harmonic analysis, voltage fluctuation alerts, and electricity auditing.
pqms.swScenarioText: Can be used for remote power quality monitoring and management in places such as industrial parks, data centers, and hospitals, enabling harmonic analysis, online optimization of compensation parameters, and automatic generation of compliance reports.
pqms.swFeature0Label: Comprehensive monitoring of up to 50th order harmonics
pqms.swFeature0Desc: Real-time collection of voltage, current, power factor, and harmonics up to the 50th order, accurately capturing each harmonic component, and quickly locating problematic equipment and pollution sources.
pqms.swFeature1Desc: Map-based display of site health status, providing an overview of the entire network's power quality distribution at a glance, with a unified analysis center for data from multiple monitoring points.
pqms.swFeature1Point1: Distribution of power quality across the entire network
pqms.swFeature2Desc: Online adjustment of SVG/APF compensation parameters, reducing on-site inspection frequency by over 90%, supporting remote OTA firmware upgrades for field devices.
pqms.swFeature3Desc: One-click export of daily/weekly/monthly reports to meet grid company compliance inspection requirements. Historical analysis reports help identify root causes of power quality degradation.
pqms.swFeature3Point2: Historical root cause analysis of degradation

vpp.title: VPP
vpp.description: VPP aggregates distributed energy storage, PV, and EV chargers to participate in grid peak shaving and frequency regulation, unlocking additional revenue.
vpp.swScenarioText: Can be used by VPP operators to aggregate multi-brand distributed energy storage devices, enabling batch scheduling and real-time status monitoring via cloud APIs, and participating in grid ancillary service markets for revenue.
vpp.swFeature0Desc: Unified scheduling of distributed energy storage, photovoltaics, and charging piles, with unlimited aggregation scale. Supports mixed deployment of heterogeneous brands and has integrated cloud APIs of mainstream energy storage brands.
vpp.swFeature0Point2: Mainstream cloud APIs have been integrated
vpp.swFeature1Desc: Scheduling response speed meets the highest requirements for grid ancillary services, supports second-level batch command dispatch, real-time status subscription: SOC, power, alarm status pushed at millisecond level.
vpp.swFeature2Desc: VPP operators do not need to develop their own access layer. AlwaysControl's unified scheduling interface supports charge/discharge commands, SOC queries, alarm subscriptions, complete scheduling records and auditing.
vpp.swFeature2Point1: Full Coverage of Charge/Discharge + Alarm Subscription
vpp.swFeature3Desc: Supports connecting to upstream grid signals or custom scheduling strategies, meeting different electricity market trading models, flexibly adapting to local regulatory requirements.

hwDesc.neuron-iii: Dedicated Controller for Charging Stations (Built-in Meter + DLB)

swScenarios.hems: Supports comprehensive management services for new energy power stations, including data acquisition, station monitoring, operation, and maintenance for applications like photovoltaics and energy storage. Through a cloud-based big data analytics platform, it helps users achieve transparent management, automated operation and maintenance, intelligent diagnostics, and assisted decision-making for all their new energy power stations. It fully meets users' needs at various levels throughout the power station lifecycle, maximizing station value and protecting core assets.
swScenarios.ess: Can be used for unified monitoring and dispatch of multi-site energy storage assets in factories, commercial buildings, campuses, etc. It enables cross-brand device integration, automatic peak-valley arbitrage, and demand management, reducing overall electricity costs by 20~40%.
swScenarios.evcms: Can be used for dynamic load balancing and operational management of multi-pile charging stations in parking lots, office buildings, campuses, etc. It enables coordinated charging and intelligent billing for multiple piles without the need for main line capacity expansion.
swScenarios.vpp: Can be used by VPP operators to aggregate multi-brand distributed energy storage devices. It enables batch dispatch and real-time status monitoring through cloud APIs, allowing participation in grid ancillary service markets for revenue generation.
swScenarios.pqms: Can be used for remote monitoring and management of power quality in industrial parks, data centers, hospitals, and other facilities. It enables harmonic analysis, online optimization of compensation parameters, and automatic generation of compliance reports.

about.title: About Us
about.subtitle: AlwaysControl — A Pioneer in Smart Management and Control for the New Energy Sector
about.companyIntroText: AlwaysControl Technology was founded in 2018 and is headquartered in Bao'an District, Shenzhen. The company focuses on smart management and control technology in the new energy sector, dedicated to providing efficient, stable, and intelligent energy management solutions to customers worldwide. After years of growth, AlwaysControl has become an industry-leading solution provider, with products and services covering over 50 countries and regions, compatible with more than 200 device brands, and over 500 projects delivered.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.subtitle: We value your privacy
privacy.content: AlwaysControl Technology (hereinafter referred to as "we") attaches great importance to user privacy. This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, store, and protect your personal information. We only collect your personal information to the extent necessary, including but not limited to your name, email address, phone number, and company name. This information is used to respond to your inquiries, provide technical support, and improve our products and services. We employ industry-standard security measures to protect your information. Without your consent, we will not share your personal information with third parties. You have the right to access, correct, or delete your personal information at any time. For any privacy-related questions, please contact us at info@alwayscontrol.com.cn.
